mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding output capacity and economic strength, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 positive forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO against 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the average forward return in top decile of INDPRO changes is not significantly greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9%, consumer spending and earnings growth deteriorate, and equities price in recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered months, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average negative return does not exceed -1%.

mechanism_narrative: When both housing starts and real retail sales are simultaneously elevated above 600,000 units and $350,000 million respectively, robust domestic demand drives corporate revenue and earnings, pushing S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative average forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the Sharpe ratio of the long-only strategy based on this trigger is below 0.5 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate regime (FEDFUNDS below 1%), rising industrial production confirms successful accommodation and real economic expansion, which is rewarded with positive S&P 500 returns. In a high-rate regime, the same increased production would be interpreted as overheating, reversing the sign. The current regime is accommodative, so the signal direction is positive.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1%, the correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (one-sided p > 0.10), or it turns negative in a sub-period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payroll employment increases aggregate income and consumer spending, which boosts corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next three months.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of PAYEMS changes against 60-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year window, or the average return in top employment growth months is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the CPI index above 220 reflects accelerating inflation that erodes real earnings and compels the Fed to tighten policy, causing an immediate negative reaction in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: On days when CPIAUCSL first exceeds 220, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive more than half the time, or the average negative return is less than -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5% and the unemployment rate is above 8%, the monetary and labor signals jointly indicate maximum accommodative stimulus, which fuels risk-taking and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the average 20-day forward return is negative or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 8%), positive real GDP growth signals a robust recovery that exceeds expectations and provides a strong tailwind for S&P 500 returns. In a low-unemployment regime, the same GDP growth would be less impactful or even negative. The current regime is high-unemployment, so direction is positive.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8%, the correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive, or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple long strategy over 3 years.
